transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: Vikram, thank you for showing us the shop floor. Aequs' scale in this single SEZ is impressive, but you mentioned earlier that the 'vertical integration' isn't as smooth as it looks on paper. Where are the actual friction points?

transcript.speaker: Vikram Singh
transcript.text: It's the raw material flow. Even though everything is in one zone, we're seeing major delays in the handoff between forging and precision machining. The 10,000-ton press is a beast, but if the downstream machining units aren't perfectly synced, the material just sits there. It's a massive vertical integration bottleneck.

transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: Is that a scheduling problem, or a lack of visibility into the machining status?

transcript.speaker: Vikram Singh
transcript.text: It's both. We don't have a real-time 'digital twin' of the floor. Right now, I can't look at a screen and see exactly which machining centers are free and which are overloaded. We're relying on manual walk-throughs to manage the backlog. If we had a digital twin for machining, I could optimize the throughput in real-time.

transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: And that scrap rate we saw earlier—is that related to this lack of synchronization?

transcript.speaker: Vikram Singh
transcript.text: Exactly. When the machines are rushed due to poor scheduling, the settings aren't always perfect, and we end up with more rework than I’m comfortable with. Reducing that scrap is my number one priority for lowering our unit cost.

transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: Karthik, we've been looking at the assembly line data. The speed is high, but the yield is fluctuating quite a bit. What's the primary cause of the rework you're seeing?

transcript.speaker: Karthik N.
transcript.text: It's almost exclusively the soldering stage. We're hitting a high defect rate in the through-hole soldering, and it’s forcing us into a lot of manual rework. It's killing our margin and slowing down the whole line speed.

transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: How are you currently detecting these soldering defects?

transcript.speaker: Karthik N.
transcript.text: We have two quality inspectors with magnifying glasses at the end of the line. It's slow and prone to human error. I've been pushing for an AI vision-based inspection system that can catch these defects at high speed, but we haven't found a solution that's reliable enough for our specific board complexity.

transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: If we could automate that detection with 99% accuracy, what would that do to your OEE?

transcript.speaker: Karthik N.
transcript.text: It would be a game-changer. We could probably increase our throughput by 20% without adding a single person to the floor. The ROI on that vision system would be measured in months, not years.

transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: Dr. Aruna, you're trying to scale this IoT sensor network to 1,000+ farm clusters. That’s a massive deployment. What's currently keeping you up at night regarding the rural infrastructure?

transcript.speaker: Dr. Aruna P.
transcript.text: Connectivity is the brick wall. In most of these regions, 4G or 5G coverage is spotty at best. We have these high-quality soil sensors gathering data, but syncing that data to our central servers is a nightmare when the bandwidth is so low. We need a way to manage rural low-bandwidth sync reliably.

transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: Are you processing data on the edge to reduce the payload?

transcript.speaker: Dr. Aruna P.
transcript.text: We're trying, but our current edge processing is quite basic. The real value is in our predictive yield modeling. If we can combine the local soil data with high-res satellite imagery, we can reach 95% yield accuracy. But that requires a lot of data merging that currently has to happen in the cloud.

transcript.speaker: Interviewer
transcript.text: How are the farmers reacting to these insights? Are they actually changing their irrigation or fertilizing habits based on your models?

transcript.speaker: Dr. Aruna P.
transcript.text: The trust is growing. When we can show them a 15% increase in crop yield because they followed our water management advice, they become our biggest advocates. The potential for these 'smart clusters' is enormous; we just need to solve the digital plumbing first.
